task_yaml.task:
I need to make a paper-reading HTML blog on the topic of Diffusion Models for Video Generation.
Please organize it around the following main thread:
1. Why video generation is harder than image generation
  - Explain the challenges of extending image generation to video generation: temporal consistency, motion modeling, long-range dependencies, and compute cost.

2. Diffusion parameterization and sampling
  - Introduce the basic modeling approach of video diffusion.
  - Explain how noise / data / velocity prediction and other parameterizations affect training and sampling stability.

3. Architectures for spatiotemporal generation
  - Explain how 3D U-Net, space-time U-Net, and similar designs model space and time jointly.

4. Scaling text-to-video systems
  - Discuss large-scale text-to-video systems such as Imagen Video and Make-A-Video.
  - Focus on cascaded generation, text conditioning, and strategies for using image/video data.

5. Latent video diffusion
  - Explain why pixel-space video diffusion is expensive.
  - Discuss how latent video diffusion reduces the cost of high-resolution video generation.

6. Adapting image diffusion models to video
  - Discuss methods such as Tune-A-Video and Text2Video-Zero that transfer image diffusion models to video.

7. Practical synthesis
  - Summarize the keys to video diffusion: stable parameterization, spatiotemporal architecture, latent representations, and image-to-video adaptation.

The candidate papers are all in `/tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/papers/`, and I renamed the files to non-semantic numbers, so do not guess from filenames. Read these papers and select the papers that should be discussed for each section, including the opening problem introduction.

Make the blog rich in both text and figures. You may screenshot figures/tables from the papers, but the screenshots must be complete, clear, matched to the content, and detailed enough to read text and details. Formulas may be written as screenshots or LaTeX. Use the prepared `/tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/assets/`, which contains the CSS, JavaScript, and MathJax formula-rendering dependencies needed by the page template.

Save the final blog to `/tmp_workspace/results/diffusion_video_blog/`; it should contain the HTML file and corresponding assets. Put images referenced by the HTML into `/tmp_workspace/clawbench/results/assets/images`, and cite the source paper for every figure in the blog.

Also save `/tmp_workspace/results/selection_note.md` explaining why each section selected these papers.

prompt:
I need to make a paper-reading HTML blog on the topic of Diffusion Models for Video Generation.
Please organize it around the following main thread:
1. Why video generation is harder than image generation
  - Explain the challenges of extending image generation to video generation: temporal consistency, motion modeling, long-range dependencies, and compute cost.

2. Diffusion parameterization and sampling
  - Introduce the basic modeling approach of video diffusion.
  - Explain how noise / data / velocity prediction and other parameterizations affect training and sampling stability.

3. Architectures for spatiotemporal generation
  - Explain how 3D U-Net, space-time U-Net, and similar designs model space and time jointly.

4. Scaling text-to-video systems
  - Discuss large-scale text-to-video systems such as Imagen Video and Make-A-Video.
  - Focus on cascaded generation, text conditioning, and strategies for using image/video data.

5. Latent video diffusion
  - Explain why pixel-space video diffusion is expensive.
  - Discuss how latent video diffusion reduces the cost of high-resolution video generation.

6. Adapting image diffusion models to video
  - Discuss methods such as Tune-A-Video and Text2Video-Zero that transfer image diffusion models to video.

7. Practical synthesis
  - Summarize the keys to video diffusion: stable parameterization, spatiotemporal architecture, latent representations, and image-to-video adaptation.

Make the blog rich in both text and figures. You may screenshot figures/tables from the papers, but the screenshots must be complete, clear, matched to the content, and detailed enough to read text and details. Formulas may be written as screenshots or LaTeX. Use the prepared `/tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/assets/`, which contains the CSS, JavaScript, and MathJax formula-rendering dependencies needed by the page template.

Save the final blog to `/tmp_workspace/results/diffusion_video_blog/`; it should contain the HTML file and corresponding assets. Put images referenced by the HTML into `/tmp_workspace/clawbench/results/assets/images`, and cite the source paper for every figure in the blog.
